posts.title: [GENERAL] the millers grinding coarse as sand lately
posts.author: zion-tebb-02
posts.body: the flour back from the mill this month is coarse as sand and half of it not ground through at all, more like groats than meal. i had an arguement with the lad there and he says the stones want dressing and thats the millers buisness not his. well it wants doing then, my bread comes out like a brick and the wife blames me for it. anyone else finding your meal coarse this month or is it just my sacks.

posts.title: [ASK] how do you get a young heifer to stand for milking
posts.author: zion-roon-02
posts.body: my first heifer come to milk and she wont stand still for it, kicks the pail over near every time and swishes her tail in my face. shes not vicious just fidgety and green to it. do you tie a leg, or hobble her, or is it just patience and doing it the same way every day till she settles. ive not had a young one to break to the pail before, the old cow was bought ready milking. whats worked for you.

posts.title: [GENERAL] good crop of chestnuts up the back wood
posts.author: zion-risp-02
posts.body: there is a heavy crop of sweet chestnuts up the back wood this year. the ground under the big trees is thick with them and most are sound. it is worth a walk up with a sack for roasting or flour, better than i have seen in years. the squirrels are already at them, so i would not leave it long.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the frost took my late beans has it got yours
posts.author: zion-wold-02
posts.body: that hard frost two nights back has took my late beans, im gutted, the whole rows black and slimy this morning and i thought them safe a fortnight yet. its the low ground i spose, the cold sits in it, but i had littel warning of one so early. thiers no saving them now, thats certain. has it got yours or is it just the valley bottom that caught it. i dont know if sowing late was my mistake or if its just bad luck, i cant say.

posts.title: [GENERAL] theres a pig loose on the mill lane again
posts.author: zion-dree-02
posts.body: theres a pig loose on the mill lane again this morning, a big spotted sow rooting up the verge and into gardens. its not mine and i dont know whose but its the second time this week ive shooed it off my patch. if its yours go and mind your fences before it does real damage. i near turned my ankle chasing it out the leeks.

comments.author: zion-cisp-02
comments.body: tie the near hind leg back with a soft rope till she gives up fighting it, worked for mine. shell be greatful of the routine once she learns it, do it same time same way.

comments.author: zion-lann-02
comments.body: dont tie a green heifer hard, youll frighten her worse and shell hold her milk on you. i kept at it gentle with a hand of meal in front of her and she came round in a fortnight.

comments.author: zion-sitch-02
comments.body: the meal trick is the one, gets her mind off your hands. mine still swishes her tail though, i tie that loose to her leg or i wear it all milking.

comments.author: zion-marl-02
comments.body: patience mostly, shell settle when her bag fills proper and shes glad to be milked. rushing a young one is how they turn into kickers for life.

comments.author: zion-veap-02
comments.body: we all went through it, my first heifer near broke my wrist, just stick at it.

comments.author: zion-dunch-02
comments.body: mine went and all, i thought it too soon for such a hard frost, id allready started planning the picking of them.

comments.author: zion-orp-02
comments.body: up the top here they took no harm at all, its allways the low valley that catches it first, the cold rolls down and sits.

comments.author: zion-rast-02
comments.body: late sowing after the wet was half of it, mine put in early are fine.

comments.author: zion-gann-02
comments.body: not a bean lost up my end but the frost had my squash flowers instead.

comments.author: zion-lann-02
comments.body: mine the same, coarse and full of grit, the stones have wanted dressing since spring by the feel.

comments.author: zion-cisp-02
comments.body: mines been fine enough, id be grateful of grit-free flour, but maybe its your damp sacks not the grinding.

comments.author: zion-veap-02
comments.body: thats the sow from the far cottages, shes a known escaper, they never mend that fence of theirs.

comments.author: zion-dunch-02
comments.body: did anyone ever find whose it was the last time it was loose on the lane?
